2026-03-23.label: Fitness Test: Critical Swim Speed (CSS)
2026-03-23.description:
Workout type: Swim
Use the results from this test to personalise your planned swim sessions.

Warm up:
6 x (50 Steady, 20s rests),
4 x (25 FS Hard + 25 FS Steady, 20s rests).

-----------
TEST:
1 x 400 FS maximal effort time trial, RECORD TIME,
5 mins Easy for active recovery,
1 x 200 FS maximal effort time trial, RECORD TIME,
1 min rest
-----------

Main Set:
1 x 100 FS Easy, 15s rest,
1 x 100 FS Steady, 15s rest,
1 x 100 FS Moderately Hard, 15s rest,
1 x 100 FS Hard, 60s rest,
4 x 50 FS Build Easy to Hard
60 secs rest.

Warm Down:
200 Choice, Steady

Speed

1. Profile picture → Settings → Zones

2. Select Pace/Speed → Activity→ Swim

3. Enter your CSS from today’s test as your threshold: https://www.myprocoach.net/calculators/critical-swim-speed/

4. No need to add zones (if you do, use MyProCoach Auto-Calculator)

----------------------------------------------
Learn more about setting your pool length and following your swim workouts:
Planned Time: 0:42
Distance Planned: 2166 m

2026-03-24.label: Fitness Test: FTP and Threshold HR
2026-03-24.description:
Workout type: Bike
Use the results from this test to calculate your thresholds, zones, and personalise your training.

Warm Up:
12 mins Steady
5 x (15 secs Hard + 45 secs Easy).

Main Set:
20 mins as hard as you can sustain.

Warm Down:
5 mins Easy

Power

1. Profile picture → Settings → Zones

2. Select Power → Activity→ Bike

3. Enter your average power from today’s test multiplied by 95% as your threshold (FTP)

4. No need to add zones (if you do, use MyProCoach Auto-Calculator)

❤️ Heart Rate ❤️ (optional):

1. Select Heart Rate → Activity→ Bike.

2. Enter your average HR less 6 beats from today’s test as the threshold.

3. Use MyProCoach Auto-Calculator to learn your training zones.
Planned Time: 0:42

2026-03-25.label: Rest Day
2026-03-25.description:
Workout type: Day Off
Allows for recovery whilst promoting adaptation to previous training stresses. Helps you get faster and keep injuries at bay.

2026-03-26.label: Fitness Test: Threshold Pace and HR
2026-03-26.description:
Workout type: Run
Use the results from this test to calculate your thresholds and personalise your training.

Warm Up:
5 mins Steady,
4 x (30 secs accelerating from Steady to Hard + 30 secs Easy recoveries).

Main Set:
Run at your fastest sustainable pace for 30 minutes. Record your average pace and heart rate.

Warm Down:
5 mins Easy/Steady.

Your Threshold Run Pace is your average pace for the 30-minute test interval.

Your Run Threshold Heart Rate is your average for the 30-minute test.

‍♂️ Pace ‍♂️

1. Profile picture → Settings → Zones

2. Select Pace/Speed → Activity→ Run

3. Enter your average pace from today’s test as your threshold

4. No need to add zones (if you do, use MyProCoach Auto-Calculator)

❤️ Heart Rate ❤️ (optional):

1. Select Heart Rate → Activity→ Run.

2. Enter your average HR from today’s test as the threshold.

3. Use MyProCoach Auto-Calculator to learn your training zones.
Planned Time: 0:44

2026-03-27.label: Pool Swim: Speed Endurance
2026-03-27.description:
Workout type: Swim
These swims involve effort at or above race pace, with regular recoveries. They will build your sustainable speed for race day.

Warm Up:
1 x (100 FS in Z2 + 100 Kick in Z2 + 20 secs rest),
1 x (100 Drill* in Z2 + 100 Pull in Z2 + 20 secs rest).

Main Set:
4 x (50 FS in Z2 + 50 FS in Z3 + 20 sec rests),
4 x (50 Pull in Z2 + 50 Pull in Z4 + 20 sec rests),
4 x (50 FS in Z2 + 50 FS in Z5 + 30 sec rests).

Warm Down:
2 x (50 FS in Z2 + 50 Choice in Z2).
MyProCoach Swim Drills:

NOTE: For all swims, use the distances in the Workout Description section. Toggle yards/meters in Workout Details.
Planned Time: 0:34
Distance Planned: 1800 m

2026-03-28.label: Aerobic Endurance Ride
2026-03-28.description:
Workout type: Bike
Ride at an easy/steady intensity, mainly in Z2 today. You should be able to chat at this intensity. This ride will improve your efficiency for using fat for fuel and increase your ability to transport oxygen to your working muscles.
Phil’s Training Tip:

When you're exercising for longer than about 1 hour 20 you need to consume carbohydrate during your workouts to stop your energy levels from dropping too much. Aim for the equivalent of an energy gel every 30 minutes for training. It doesn't even have to be an "energy" product. You could have a cereal bar, a banana, a sugary drink or whatever you like. It just needs to consist mainly of carbohydrate. And water is fine for hydration.
Planned Time: 1:30

2026-03-29.label: Aerobic Endurance Run
2026-03-29.description:
Workout type: Run
A steady run, mainly in Z1-Z2. You should be able to chat at this intensity. This is your long run for the week and it will progress in duration. They will gradually build your endurance.
Phil's Training Tip:

Your pace on these runs will depend on several factors, such as the terrain, elevation and conditions. Try not to get too worried about sticking to a set pace. Heart Rate or Rate of Perceived Exertion can work well instead, helping you to listen to your body.
Planned Time: 0:45

2026-03-30.label: Pool Swim: Aerobic Endurance
2026-03-30.description:
Workout type: Swim
These sessions will be done at lower intensities, and will progress in duration. The goal is to build your endurance ensuring you can easily swim the distance on race day and still have lots of energy left for the ride and run.

Warm Up:
1 x (100 FS in Z2 + 15 secs rest),
2 x (100 Drill* in Z2 + 15 sec rests),
1 x (100 Pull in Z2 + 15 secs rest).

Main Set:
1 x (400 Pull in Z2 + 25 secs rest),
4 x (100 FS in Z3 + 25 sec rests),
2 x (200 Pull in Z2 + 20 sec rests),
2 x (100 FS in Z3 + 25 sec rests).

Warm Down:
2 x (50 Choice in Z2 + 50 FS in Z2).

2026-03-31.label: Speed Workout Bike
2026-03-31.description:
Workout type: Bike
Warm Up:
5 mins in low Z2,
5 mins in mid Z2,
5 mins in upper Z2.

Main Set:
5 mins in Z5 + 2 mins 30 secs recovery in Z1,
4 mins in Z5 + 2 mins recovery in Z1,
3 mins in Z5 + 1 min 30 secs recovery in Z1,
2 mins in Z5 + 1 min recovery in Z1,
1 min in Z5 + 1 min recovery in Z1.

Warm Down:
10 mins in Z2.
Speed workouts will develop your power, economy and skill. Over time, they will prepare your body for the demands of pedalling harder and faster.
Planned Time: 0:48

2026-04-01.label: Speed Workout Run
2026-04-01.description:
Workout type: Run
Warm Up:
15 mins in Z2.

Main Set:
6 x (2 mins in low Z5 + 60 sec recoveries in Z1).

Warm Down:
10 mins in Z1-2.
Speed workouts will develop your leg speed and running economy. Over time, they will teach your body to cope with the demands of running faster.
Planned Time: 0:43

2026-04-04.label: Aerobic Endurance Ride
2026-04-04.description:
Workout type: Bike
Ride mainly in Z2 today.
Phil’s Training Tip:

Don't feel you need to do all your workouts alone. If you enjoy doing club or group workouts, you can still incorporate these into your training plan. In fact, it's a good idea. Just make sure that your group workouts replace similar types of workouts from your training plan. For example, you might swap solo speed work for a group/coached session. Or an aerobic endurance workout can be done with your club/team or workout buddies.
Planned Time: 1:45

2026-04-05.label: Aerobic Endurance Run
2026-04-05.description:
Workout type: Run
A nice steady run, mainly in Z1-Z2.
Phil’s Training Tip:

Don't panic if you cannot complete 100% of your workouts. Do your best to stick to your plan, while accepting that occasionally life gets in the way. Following a plan will help you train more effectively than not having a plan at all. Do your best with whatever time and resources you have, and don't give up. Don't worry about one bad week, it’s what you do consistently over a period of months that counts.
Planned Time: 0:50
